quality.rationale: 直接套用联络公式计算，逻辑链条较短但计算结构高度通用，适合作为坐标变换基础训练。

origin.question:
[文件 1: 1_s7_7.2_c.md]

Problem 7.2. For a 2-dimensional flat, Euclidean space described by polar coordinates $\mathbf{r}$ , $	heta$ , assume that the geodesics are the usual straight lines.
(c) Finally, from the line element $\mathrm{ds}^2 = \mathrm{dr}^2 +\mathrm{r}^2\mathrm{d}	heta^2$ find the Christoffel symbols, in the usual way, as derivatives of the metric coefficients $\mathbf{g}_{\mu 
u}$ . (All three methods, of course, must give the same Christoffel symbols.)
origin.solution:
(c) We now use
$$
\Gamma_{\beta \gamma}^{a} = \frac {1}{2} \left. \mathbf {g}^{a \mu} \left(\mathbf {g}_{\mu \beta , \gamma} + \mathbf {g}_{\mu \gamma , \beta} - \mathbf {g}_{\beta \gamma , \mu}\right) \right..
$$
For the metric $\mathrm{ds}^2 = \mathrm{dr}^2 +\mathrm{r}^2\mathrm{d}\theta^2$ the only nonvanishing derivative is $\mathbf{g}_{\theta \theta ,\mathbf{r}} = 2\mathbf{r}$ . Thus $\Gamma_{\beta \gamma}^{a} = 0$ unless 2 indices are $	heta$ 's and one is an $\mathbf{r}$ :
$$
\begin{array}{l} \Gamma_{\theta \theta}^{r} = g^{r r} \left(- \frac {1}{2} g_{\theta \theta , r}\right) = - r \\
\Gamma_{\mathrm {r} \theta}^{\theta} = \mathbf {g}^{\theta \theta} \left(\frac {1}{2} \mathbf {g}_{\theta \theta , \mathrm{r}}\right) = \frac {1}{\mathrm {r}}. \\
\end {array}
$$
